name: Prompting Quality Rubric
description: Evaluates the quality of prompts written by learners across CRAFT dimensions and iteration behavior

dimensions.name: Context Provision
dimensions.description: How well the user provides background information the AI needs

levels.label: Missing
levels.criteria: No context provided. Bare task with no background.
examples: 'Write a summary'
'Help me with this'

levels.label: Minimal
levels.criteria: Some context but critical details missing.
examples: 'Write a summary of this article' (no article provided, no audience specified)

levels.label: Adequate
levels.criteria: Key context present but could be more specific.
examples: 'Summarize this article about climate change for my 10th grade class'

levels.label: Strong
levels.criteria: Rich context including audience, purpose, and relevant background.
examples: 'Summarize this article for my 10th grade biology class. Students already know about the greenhouse effect. We're building toward a debate next week.'

levels.label: Expert
levels.criteria: Comprehensive context with edge case handling and explicit assumptions.
examples: Includes prior knowledge, constraints, what to do if information is ambiguous, and how to handle conflicting data

dimensions.name: Action Specificity
dimensions.description: How clearly the user defines what the AI should do

levels.label: Vague
levels.criteria: Action is unclear or overly broad.
examples: 'Help me with marketing'

levels.label: General
levels.criteria: Action identifiable but lacks specifics.
examples: 'Write marketing copy for my product'

levels.label: Clear
levels.criteria: Specific action with basic success criteria.
examples: 'Write 3 email subject lines for our new product launch'

levels.label: Precise
levels.criteria: Well-defined action with constraints and quality criteria.
examples: 'Write 3 email subject lines under 50 characters that create urgency without clickbait'

levels.label: Expert
levels.criteria: Decomposed complex tasks into clear steps with success criteria for each.
examples: Breaks a complex task into a multi-step chain with clear handoffs and verification points

dimensions.name: Format Specification
dimensions.description: How well the user defines the desired output structure

levels.label: None
levels.criteria: No format guidance.
examples: Output format entirely left to AI's default

levels.label: Implicit
levels.criteria: Format implied but not stated.
examples: 'Give me a list' (but no detail on list structure)

levels.label: Basic
levels.criteria: Format specified at a high level.
examples: 'Use bullet points and keep it under 200 words'

levels.label: Detailed
levels.criteria: Specific structure with sections and formatting.
examples: 'Use markdown with H2 headers for each section. Include a summary table at the top.'

levels.label: Expert
levels.criteria: Precise format with schema or template, including handling of edge cases.
examples: Provides JSON schema, template structure, or detailed formatting rules with fallback instructions

dimensions.name: Constraint Handling
dimensions.description: How well the user specifies boundaries, limitations, and edge case behavior

levels.label: None
levels.criteria: No constraints specified.
examples: Completely open-ended request

levels.label: Basic
levels.criteria: One or two simple constraints.
examples: 'Keep it under 500 words'

levels.label: Adequate
levels.criteria: Multiple relevant constraints.
examples: 'Under 500 words, professional tone, no jargon'

levels.label: Strong
levels.criteria: Constraints include edge case handling.
examples: 'If a data point is missing, say "not available" rather than estimating'

levels.label: Expert
levels.criteria: Comprehensive constraints including anti-hallucination instructions and fallback behaviors.
examples: Explicit handling for missing data, uncertainty, out-of-scope requests, and quality validation

dimensions.name: Iteration Quality
dimensions.description: How effectively the user improves prompts based on output

levels.label: None
levels.criteria: Accepts first output without revision.
examples: Takes whatever the AI produces

levels.label: Reactive
levels.criteria: Revises only when output is obviously wrong.
examples: 'That's wrong, try again' without specifying what to change

levels.label: Targeted
levels.criteria: Identifies specific issues and adjusts prompt accordingly.
examples: 'The tone is too formal. Make it conversational but keep the technical accuracy.'

levels.label: Strategic
levels.criteria: Systematic iteration with clear improvement goals per revision.
examples: Revises context, then format, then constraints — each iteration targets a specific dimension

levels.label: Expert
levels.criteria: Diagnostic iteration — analyzes why output fell short and adjusts the root cause.
examples: 'The output hallucinated because I didn't specify sources. Adding: cite only from the provided documents.'
